steps.title: Identify the grammatical pattern
steps.content: The sentence frame is “the rover spent over fourteen years _____ data.” The construction spent [time period] + [verb form] requires a gerund (the “-ing” form) to describe the ongoing activity that filled that stretch of time. The subject is singular (“the rover”), and the action is in the past.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘is collecting’: Present-tense progressive; the sentence describes a past activity, and “spent” cannot pair with a present-tense verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘collecting’: The gerund correctly completes “spent over fourteen years collecting data,” matching Standard English usage for the spent time doing pattern. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘were collecting’: Plural past progressive; the subject “the rover” is singular, and this form cannot follow “spent.” ✗
  • Choice D — ‘collected’: Simple past; placing two simple-past verbs side by side (“spent… collected”) breaks the idiomatic structure and creates a grammatical mismatch. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
